data.title: Cartier owner Richemont, Swatch Group slide after weak February exports
data.description:
** Shares in Swiss watchmakers Richemont and Swatch Group slip after weak exports data

** Swiss Watch exports fell in February, down 8.2% y/y in value terms compared to a 4% rise in January, despite much easier comps, J.P.Morgan analysts say in a note

** They add that all key regions globally turned negative in February

** "Overall this is a soft read on the latest trends in the watches segments, likely to weigh on Swatch and Richemont this morning" - JPM

** Cartier owner Richemont is down 2.7% at 0817 GMT, Swatch Group falls 3.8%

data.title: Hapag-Lloyd Guidance Points to Consensus EBIT Downgrades — Market Talk
data.description: Hapag-Lloyd's EBIT guidance is well below consensus so will likely lead to some near-term downward revisions, JPMorgan analysts write. The mid-point of the 2025 EBITDA guidance range of $2.5 billion-$4 billion is broadly in line with consensus at $3.2 billion, they say. However, the mid-point of the $0-$1.5 billion EBIT guidance is 17% below the $900 million expected by consensus. Outlook commentary emphasizes the fragility of the economic and geopolitical environment. Company focus in the first half of the year is on implementing the Gemini network and keeping a close eye on costs. "We remain cautious on the outlook given the rapid deterioration in container spot rates since the start of the year, despite Red Sea diversions persisting." Shares fall 4.9%. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Energy Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Energy markets. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0825 GMT - Hapag-Lloyd's EBIT guidance is well below consensus so will likely lead to some near-term downward revisions, JPMorgan analysts write. The mid-point of the 2025 EBITDA guidance range of $2.5 billion-$4 billion is broadly in line with consensus at $3.2 billion, they say. However, the mid-point of the $0-$1.5 billion EBIT guidance is 17% below the $900 million expected by consensus. Outlook commentary emphasizes the fragility of the economic and geopolitical environment. Company focus in the first half of the year is on implementing the Gemini network and keeping a close eye on costs. "We remain cautious on the outlook given the rapid deterioration in container spot rates since the start of the year, despite Red Sea diversions persisting." Shares fall 4.9%. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0744 GMT - RWE's cut to capital expenditures and targets for higher returns ought to be well received, RBC Capital Markets analyst Alexander Wheeler says in a research note. The German energy company reiterating its medium-term targets should also be seen as a positive, the analyst says. Consensus expectations for this year are in line with RWE's targeted range for adjusted net profit and adjusted Ebitda, the analyst says. The company said that it would be more strict with its investment criteria, translating to 10 billion fewer euros planned to be spent in the 2025 to 2030 period than previously planned. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

0707 GMT - Comex Gold futures' uptrend is still intact, according to the daily chart, RHB Retail Research's Joseph Chai says in a research report. Despite the precious metal posting a 'Doji' candlestick that indicates neutral market sentiment on Wednesday, bullish momentum remains reflected by the upward-sloping relative strength index, the analyst says. "We anticipate the positive price action to follow through for the immediate session," says Chai, who adds that the precious metal is on track to test the $3,100/oz level. On the downside, immediate support is at $2,900/oz, followed by subsequent support at $2,850/oz. Spot gold edges 0.1% lower to $3,046.20/oz. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Exosens to Join SBF 120 Index on Euronext Paris
data.description:
Exosens will become part of the Euronext Paris Stock Exchange's SBF 120 index, according to a Thursday release.

The French electro-optical technology company's inclusion will take effect upon the market opening on March 24.

data.title: UK's Bloomsbury Publishing gains after annual targets expected to exceed consensus
data.description:
** Shares of Bloomsbury Publishing up 7.6% at 624p- among the top gainers on FTSE 250 index

** The British publishing house says annual targets are tracking ahead of market consensus

** Stock is set for the biggest one-day pct gain since October 2024

** Market expectations for the year ending February 28 prior to Thursday's announcement was revenue of 333.4 million pounds ($432.49 million) and pre-tax profit of 39.6 million pounds

** Co says performance in the second half of the year was strong

** BLPU fell ~7% YTD

($1 = 0.7709 pounds)

data.title: Poly Property Announces Final Dividend Of HK$0.021 Per Share
data.description:
Poly Property Group Co Ltd :

  • FINAL DIVIDEND OF HK$0.021 PER SHARE

  • FY PROFIT ATTRIBUTABLE RMB182.9 MILLION

  • FY REVENUE RMB40.21 BILLION

data.title: Tech, Media & Telecom Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Technology, Media and Telecom. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

0748 GMT - Advanced Info Service appears to have an optimistic outlook for revenue growth and mobile handset margins, Maybank Securities (Thailand)'s Wasu Mattanapotchanart, citing the CFO's comments at a recent meeting. The company's mobile revenue growth is expected to outperform Thailand's GDP growth slightly over next few years partly due to rising prepaid revenue, according to the CFO. The prepaid revenue growth should be supported by personalised packages, including unlimited data and premium subscriptions for video-content applications, the analyst notes. Also, management aims to lift handset margins further by increasing the proportion of retail sales compared with wholesale revenue. The brokerage has a buy rating and a target price of THB312.00 on the shares, which are 1.5% higher at THB270.00. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0457 GMT - Tencent's AI investment strategy should propel long-term structural growth for all its business units, Daiwa analysts say in a research note. Daiwa believes Tencent is best positioned within China's internet sector to benefit from the increasing adoption of AI. Tencent's AI-powered features could drive marketing services revenue up 23% on year in 1Q, the analysts say. As the tech giant allocates more chip capacity to external clients, the analysts expect fintech and business services revenue growth to accelerate to 10% on year in 1Q. Daiwa lifts its 2025-2026 Tencent earnings per share estimate by 4%-5% to reflect a better outlook. Daiwa raises the stock's target to HK$670.00 from HK$565.00 and maintains a buy call. Shares are last at HK$526.00.(sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0236 GMT - Tencent could further expand its user base by introducing AI solutions to various consumer-facing products, Citi analysts say in a research note. As constraints over graphic processing units gradually ease, following its stepped-up capital expenditure investment, Tencent's cloud revenue could accelerate in 2025, the analysts say. Citi raises Tencent's 2025 revenue and earnings estimates by 3.3% and 7.7%, respectively, reflecting the company's latest results. Citi maintains a buy rating on Tencent and raises its target price to HK$681.00 from HK$648.00, citing its solid execution track record, steady gaming profit, and likely rising revenue from its ad and business services, boosted its AI adoption. Shares are last down 2.4% at HK$527.00. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0211 GMT - Tencent could become a dominant AI player in China given its unparalleled breadth and depth of social and user insight, Barclays analysts say in a research note. They note Tencent had a bit of a late start in the AI race, but say it has generally caught up with the rest of China's AI leaders. Tencent's around $5 billion capital expenditure in 4Q should build a foundation for strong cloud and AI growth in 2025, the analysts say. Meanwhile, Tencent's consistent around $20 billion annual shareholder return through dividends and buybacks signals that management and shareholder interests are aligned, the bank says. Barclays raises Tencent ADR target price to US$88.00 from US$66.00. Shares last closed 0.1% higher at US$69.59. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0157 GMT - The merger of Axiata Group and Sinar Mas's Indonesian telecom operations should strengthen market position, but integration costs over the next one to two years may weigh on earnings, Kenanga IB analyst Kylie Chan Sze Zan says in a note. Also, the merged XL Axiata and Smartfren entity could face pressure on growth in average revenue per user due to inflation and competition from larger peers, particularly Telkomsel, she reckons. Axiata may face near-term earnings dilution as its stake in Indonesian unit XL drops to 47.9% from 66.5%, she adds. Kenanga cuts Axiata's target price to MYR1.85 from MYR2.05 while maintaining a market perform rating on the stock. Shares are unchanged at MYR1.78.(yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

1755 GMT - The EU's warning is "a strong signal to Alphabet that its approach needs to change rapidly," Emmanuel Mounier, secretary general at travel group association EU Travel Tech, says. The European Commission said on Wednesday that Google appears to be favoring its own services--like Google Shopping and hotel booking--over rivals' offerings. Mounier, whose group counts companies like Booking.com and Skyscanner among its members, says the EU is at a "turning point" with its DMA enforcement. "By ending Google Search self-preferencing, it can demonstrate that it can deliver real benefits to all digital businesses, including U.S. ones, as well as European consumers," he says. (edith.hancock@wsj.com)

1745 GMT - Telecommunications heavyweight América Móvil plans to pay dividends of 0.52 Mexican pesos a share, while adding MXN10 billion to its share-buyback fund for the April 2025 to April 2026 period. The proposal puts the dividend yield at 3.6% at current prices, plus about 1.1% if the buyback fund is used up, analysts at Banorte say in a note. The implications are neutral for the stock, they say. "The company's outlook remains favorable, back by sustained demand and good growth despite a challenging ecoomic environment in coming quarters." América Móvil shares are down 0.9% on the Mexican stock exchange.(anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1740 GMT - The EU's enforcement of its new tech antitrust rulebook "has been marked by opaque, discretionary process that lacks predictability and proportionality," Daniel Friedlaender, senior vice president and head of tech lobby group CCIA Europe, says. The bloc's competition watchdog moved forward with its scrutiny of Apple and Google under the Digital Markets Act. CCIA counts both Apple and Google among its members, alongside other tech giants like Meta and Amazon, which also have to comply with the law. "The commission's apparent push to impose granular design changes on digital products, impacting the user experience and security, raises questions about whether all the tradeoffs for consumers and business users have been properly considered," he says.(edith.hancock@wsj.com)

1640 GMT - Rents in the top 50 U.S. metros have dropped for over a year, but low multifamily permitting--294,000 units in 2024 versus 318,000 at the 2020 pandemic peak--may drive future increases, according to Realtor.com. Rent surged during the pandemic, followed by a 19-month decline amid ongoing construction, dampening builder enthusiasm. The U.S. faces a 3.8 million home shortage, with multifamily vacancy rates rising as builders adjust to economic shifts. But, limited permitting in high-demand markets with climbing rents, like New York, Kansas City, and Detroit--where 2024 permitting fell below recent norms--could tighten supply and boost rents further. Nine of the top 50 metros saw both reduced permitting and rising rents in 2024, signaling potential future growth. While multifamily construction has eased some pressure, regional shortfalls and low permitting in key areas may reverse the rent decline. (chris.wack@wsj.com)

1314 GMT - President Trump's media company is making its way to Roku. Trump Media & Technology Group says streaming platform Truth+ has successfully launched an app for the streaming-service provider's TVs. Trump Media, parent company best known for the Truth Social platform, says the app will initially provide access to live TV channels with video-on-demand content coming in the near future. Trump Media also says it plans to introduce apps for Truth+ to other connected TV platforms including Samsung and LG. Shares of Trump Media tick up 1.2% to $20.34 premarket. (denny.jacob@wsj.com; @pennedbyden)

1104 GMT - Google's acquisition of Wiz could spur further consolidation in the cybersecurity space, with large players acquiring more point solutions and leaning further into platform approaches, says Susquehanna's Shyam Patil in a research note. The analyst says that the deal could potentially change the nature of Google's relationships with other partners like Palo Alto Networks, which has its own cloud security offerings that compete directly with Wiz. Patil adds that Google emphasized the acquisition will make cybersecurity more accessible and simpler for all types of organizations, regardless of business size or vertical. (denny.jacob@wsj.com; @pennedbyden)

0828 GMT - Singapore Technologies Engineering is likely entering a multi-year earnings upcycle, CGS International analysts say in a research report. The technology, defense and engineering group expects 2024-2029 net profit CAGR to exceed revenue CAGR by up to 5 percentage points. The brokerage's analysts cite a better operating leverage and an improved sales mix for their forecast. The Singapore-listed company's 2029 defense revenue target of S$7.5 billion was also a positive surprise, the brokerage says, adding that it sees this as a reflection of the company's confidence in its growth prospects. CGS International raises the stock's target price to S$7.40 from S$5.60 with an unchanged add rating. Shares are 2.2% higher at S$6.52. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Health Care Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering the Health Care sector. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

0721 GMT - Bangkok Dusit Medical Services is a pick for stability in Thailand's healthcare sector, Thanachart Securities' Siriporn Arunothai says in a research report. One factor is that the company's hospital portfolio covers mid- and high-income segments, which makes it less vulnerable to slower economic growth, the analyst says. Another is that the hospital network operator has 28% exposure to foreign patient revenue, which is growing more rapidly than Thai patient revenue, the analyst says. A third factor is that its foreign patient base is diversified, implying smaller impact from any declines in Kuwaiti patient flows. The brokerage maintains the stock's buy rating and target price of THB34.00. Shares are 0.4% lower at THB23.50. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0231 GMT - Malaysian consumer sector growth could be positive in 2025, driven by wage hikes, robust economic conditions, and government subsidies supporting lower-income groups, RHB IB analysts Soong Wei Siang and Tai Yu Jie say in a note. This trend appears to be unfolding, with most companies spoken to experiencing positive momentum in their quarterly numbers, they say. However, inflation risks from petrol subsidy reforms may remain uncertain, they note. RHB maintains a neutral rating on Malaysia's consumer products sector, and peg Mr. D.I.Y. Group (M), Guan Chong, Farm Fresh and Focus Point as top picks. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

1449 GMT - The potential impact of any federal funding cuts to domestic HIV prevention on Gilead's pre-exposure prophylaxis, or PrEP, business is likely to be minimal, according to Citi analysts. Citi notes that Gilead has stated that the PrEP market is currently 80% commercial and only 20% reliant on government channels, suggesting that even with significant funding cuts, the majority of Gilead's PrEP revenue would remain intact. Gilead also expects strong commercial uptake of lenacapavir, its next-generation PrEP drug, and Citi says it's a buyer of Gilead stock on any weakness ahead of the upcoming lenacapavir PrEP launch in mid-2025. Citi maintains its buy rating and $125 target price on Gilead shares, which are off 3.9% to $105.97. (colin.kellaher@wsj.com)

1439 GMT - Gilead Sciences are weaker following news the Health and Human Services Department is considering cutting funding for HIV prevention, but analysts believe that threat poses only moderate risk. Potential cuts to CDC funding would not limit access to pre-exposure prophylaxis, a preventative treatment for HIV patients, because the organization only works on research, not providing the drugs, JPMorgan analysts say. The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, which is part of HHS, spent about $1.3 billion in HIV funding as of 2023, BMO analyst Evan David Seigerman says, the removal of which "should only represent a modest headwind" to Gilead. Gilead is off 3.7%. (katherine.hamilton@wsj.com)

1420 GMT - Tuesday's 27% selloff in Sarepta Therapeutics after the death of a patient who received its Elevidys gene therapy makes for a compelling buying opportunity, according to Mizuho. In a note, Mizuho's Uy Ear says Sarepta's current stock price captures little value for Elevidys, as the value of Sarepta's exon-skipping for Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy and limb-girdle muscular dystrophy businesses add up to about $70 a share. Mizuho, with an outperform rating and $190 price target on Sarepta, says Sarepta management is standing by its 2025 guidance, and the analysts still see the Elevidys benefit/risk profile as positive. Sarepta up 6.3% to $78.09. (colin.kellaher@wsj.com)

data.title: French Bank Credit Mutuel to Buy Germany's OLB
data.description:
By Elena Vardon

France's Credit Mutuel Alliance Federale agreed to buy northern German bank Oldenburgische Landesbank.

The French cooperative lender will buy OLB through its subsidiary, Targo Deutschland, in a bid to create the 10th largest bank in terms of assets in Germany, which is Credit Mutuel's second-largest market, it said Thursday.

OLB had been considering an initial public listing but by favoring a sale, its shareholders decided to discontinue those plans. An acquisition is expected to benefit the group more in the long term, the German lender said in a separate statement.

The transaction is subject to approvals and is expected to close in the first half of 2026.

The banks didn't disclose financial details.

Write to Elena Vardon at elena.vardon@wsj.com

data.title: Financial Services Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Financial Services. Exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

0354 GMT - ANZ Group replaces National Australia Bank as Morgan Stanley analysts' top pick among Australia's major banks. Both lenders are rated equal-weight by MS analysts, but they highlight ANZ's wider-than-usual discount to peers following its share-price underperformance through 2024. They tell clients in a note that they also prefer ANZ for its differentiated institutional bank, positive momentum in Australian mortgages, good cost discipline and its much-improved risk profile. They have some concerns about ANZ's relatively low provision coverage and limited flexibility on capital management, but raise their target price 7.7% to A$29.30. Shares are up 1.7% at A$29.22. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

0352 GMT - National Australia Bank's senior-management changes raise the lender's execution risk and prompt Morgan Stanley analysts to lower their rating on the stock to equal-weight from overweight. They also worry about pressure on margins from competition in business banking, lower-than-market growth rates in mortgage lending, and less chance of further capital-management initiatives. They tell clients in a note that the factors will likely weigh on near-term share-price performance. However, NAB still has a solid recent track record on costs and healthy levels of provisions, they add. MS cuts its target price 7.0% to A$34.80. Shares are up 1.2% at A$33.05. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

1644 GMT - BBVA's hostile takeover bid for smaller peer Sabadell looks like a "shaky" transaction due to the strong negative social reaction in Spain and potential no-merger scenario the suitor is considering, Sabadell's CEO says at a London conference. If a merger doesn't go through, BBVA is envisaging a scenario under which the two separate banks operate under one entity. This structure would make it impossible to achieve the targeted synergies, Sabadell's CEO Cesar Gonzalez Bueno says. The competition authority CNMC, which is currently undertaking a phase 2 review, probably won't take any hard stance on the deal given their methodology, which is based on solving competition issues with behavioral elements, as opposed to structural ones, he adds. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

1511 GMT - An expected rebound in M&A activity after two weak years has yet to materialize so far in 2025, Oppenheimer analysts Chris Kotowski and Kevin Tripp say in a research note. Announced M&A is up just 2.4% year-to-date, they say. Current uncertainty over tariffs, cuts to government spending and "general upheaval of 80 years of trade and security agreements," are all expected to keep M&A activity constrained, the analysts say. There had been optimism entering the new year that there would be pent-up demand for M&A after a "relatively moribund" 2023 and 2024 for dealmaking volumes, they say. Interest rates had peaked, credit spreads had tightened and stock valuations were generally strong, further fueling the optimism, the analysts add. Given the scenario Oppenheimer downgrades Carlyle Group, Goldman Sachs and Jefferies to perform ratings. (dean.seal@wsj.com)

1407 GMT - In 4Q, U.S. renter households grew 0.8% year-over-year to 45.4 million, the slowest rise since 1Q 2023, while homeowner households also increased 0.8% to 86.9 million, according to Redfin. This marks the first time in over a year both grew at the same rate, after four quarters of faster renter growth. Home prices are 40% above pre-pandemic levels, versus 20% for rents, driving a rising rentership rate. Increased rental supply has curbed rent growth. Nationally, 34.3% rent and 65.7% own, stable figures, but rentership spikes in costly coastal metros like New York, Los Angeles and San Francisco. Wealthy renters dominate pricey coastal markets, Redfin says.(chris.wack@wsj.com)

1349 GMT - BBVA is confident that the competition authorities will approve its bid for smaller peer Sabadell, which is currently under phase 2 review, the Spanish bank's CEO says at a London conference. "We are in the last stages of that process... Our best case scenario is that in the next few weeks we will have the conclusion of the final decision of the [Spanish competition regulator] CNMC," Onur Genc says. The bank submitted what the executive calls an unprecedented and powerful list of remedies around volumes and prices guarantees, among others, to the regulator to address the concerns that have been raised around the combination. BBVA is confident that it will get the green light from the regulator. "We have no doubt that Sabadell shareholders will find a better home in BBVA," he adds. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

1300 GMT - It could take until 2027 for UniCredit to be convinced that the rally on Commerzbank's shares is deserved and pave the way for a deal, the Italian bank's CEO says at a Morgan Stanley conference in London. It is taking longer than initially anticipated to move forward with its potential takeover plans for the German bank, Andrea Orcel says. It is now a "different transaction" compared to when it first built up a 9% stake in September, he says. "Now we have a unique silver lining from the elongation of this project, which is as a shareholder we're booking a significant gain" on its investment, which now stands at 28%, Orcel says. "We're very comfortable in waiting," he says, adding the bank is focused on executing its plan which might include the integration of Banco BPM if that deal goes through.(elena.vardon@wsj.com)

1141 GMT - Mediobanca will evaluate how to use its excess capital and its stake in insurer Generali for M&A deals once there is clarity on the outcome of the takeover bid from Banca Monte dei Paschi di Siena, the Italian bank's CEO says at a London conference. Once investors have decided whether they want to pursue the standalone route or combine with MPS, the group will try to engage to see whether there is room for consolidation, Alberto Nagel says. In its corporate and investment bank, Mediobanca could expand into new verticals and geographies only if they are considered strategic. In consumer, it will focus on bolt-on deals and fintech partnerships while it could also look at wealth-management deals, he adds. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

1123 GMT - UBS Group expects IT decommissioning to be the main driver of the remaining $5.5 billion cost savings from its acquisition of Credit Suisse, CEO Sergio Ermotti says at a Morgan Stanley conference in London. Further cost savings will come from noncore assets, real estate and headcount, but IT decommissioning will be the biggest contributor by far, Ermotti says. The bank is about to start migrating accounts of former Credit Suisse customers in Switzerland to the UBS platform, which will allow it to disconnect data centers and servers, Ermotti says. The move entails transferring 95 petabytes of data, with one petabyte being the equivalent of 500 billion pages of standard text, the UBS CEO says. The real challenge is whether the migration will be completed on time and on budget, Ermotti says. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

1121 GMT - Monte dei Paschi's hopes for synergies from its takeover offer for Mediobanca are unlikely to be achieved, Mediobanca's CEO says at a London conference. MPS's plans to cut costs seem ambitious, with less than a third of targeted savings achievable given the two Italian banks don't have overlapping branch networks, Alberto Nagel says. He also points to the different IT systems and platforms from the banks' diverging businesses and customer base. A deal would generate double-digit dilution in terms of earnings per share and dividend per share of the combined group, Nagel adds. The potential transaction isn't positive for Mediobanca or for MPS's shareholders, he notes. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

1013 GMT - UBS Group's clients are keen to diversify in a volatile environment, Chief Executive Officer Sergio Ermotti says at a Morgan Stanley conference in London. Clients are looking for advice and solutions to navigate an uncertain environment, he says. "Not surprisingly, [the] consensus that we saw towards the end of 2024 and beginning of 2025 has turned out to be totally wrong," Ermotti says. UBS's CEO says he is confident the bank is able to fulfill its targets in terms of costs, growth and returns on capital. Shares fall 1.8%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

1009 GMT - The market is ignoring some opportunities in Banca Monte dei Paschi di Siena's bid for peer Mediobanca, Deutsche Bank says in a research note. These include the leveraging of Mediobanca's balance sheet and a further acceleration of its distribution policy, analyst Giovanni Razzoli writes. "We see an asymmetric risk-reward profile in MPS at the moment," he says. MPS's valuation has adjusted to reflect a significant shift in strategy since the tender offer in January, lifting its shares though they remain at a 15% discount to peers, he notes. DB raises the rating on the stock to buy from hold and lifts its target price to 8.6 euros from 7.5 euros. MPS shares rise 3.1% to 7.86 euros and are up 16% year to date. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

data.title: Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Energy and Utilities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

0744 GMT - RWE's cut to capital expenditures and targets for higher returns ought to be well received, RBC Capital Markets analyst Alexander Wheeler says in a research note. The German energy company reiterating its medium-term targets should also be seen as a positive, the analyst says. Consensus expectations for this year are in line with RWE's targeted range for adjusted net profit and adjusted Ebitda, the analyst says. The company said that it would be more strict with its investment criteria, translating to 10 billion fewer euros planned to be spent in the 2025 to 2030 period than previously planned. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

1917 GMT - Oil futures rise in a choppy session marked by mixed U.S. inventory data and geopolitical news. The EIA reported a 1.7 million barrel increase in U.S. crude oil stocks, which was above the 500,000 barrel estimate in a Wall Street Journal survey of analysts. Gasoline stocks fell less than expected as demand eased from the week before, while distillate inventories saw an unexpected 2.8 million barrel decline. On the geopolitical front, a Russian pledge not to attack Ukrainian energy infrastructure was tempered by the idea that a ceasefire is still some time off. The Fed kept interest rates steady as expected, but lowered its growth projections while raising its inflation outlook. WTI settles up 0.4% at $67.16 a barrel, and Brent gains 0.3% to $70.78 a barrel. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1341 GMT - Oil futures hold their ground with increased Middle East tensions countered by bearish factors like Russian President Vladimir Putin's agreement not to attack Ukrainian energy facilities and OPEC+ plans to start raising ouput in less than two weeks time. The API reported a 4.6 million barrel build in U.S. crude stocks for last week, analysts note. The report was "mostly bearish, however being offset by draws in inventories at Cushing as well as gasoline and distillates," Dennis Kissler of BOK Financial says in a note. "Look for more of a choppy trade as Russian/Ukraine talks proceed." Closely watched EIA inventory data are due at 10:30 a.m. ET. WTI is off 0.1% at $66.81 a barrel and Brent is near flat at $70.54 a barrel. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1107 GMT - Canada's big energy companies argue its time to focus on building needed energy infrastructure to secure economic sovereignty for the country through the energy industry. With the country heading toward a federal election, CEOs from the 10 largest oil and gas companies and four largest pipeline operators sent an open letter to political party leaders proposing an action plan for new oil and gas pipelines and liquefied natural gas export terminals. They call for regulation to be simplified and for a commitment to firm deadlines for project approvals. The CEOs also want caps on emissions to be dropped and the federal carbon levy to be repealed to allow provincial governments to set carbon regulations. (robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbMStewart)

data.title: Basic Materials Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Basic Materials. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

0707 GMT - Comex Gold futures' uptrend is still intact, according to the daily chart, RHB Retail Research's Joseph Chai says in a research report. Despite the precious metal posting a 'Doji' candlestick that indicates neutral market sentiment on Wednesday, bullish momentum remains reflected by the upward-sloping relative strength index, the analyst says. "We anticipate the positive price action to follow through for the immediate session," says Chai, who adds that the precious metal is on track to test the $3,100/oz level. On the downside, immediate support is at $2,900/oz, followed by subsequent support at $2,850/oz. Spot gold edges 0.1% lower to $3,046.20/oz. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0249 GMT - Iron ore falls in early Asian trade amid softening sentiment due to signs of weakness in China's steel industry, the ANZ Research team writes in a note. They point to prices of futures for steel rebar, which is used for construction, hitting a six-month low. This comes as data indicates continued weakness in China's housing prices and new construction starts, they add. The most-traded iron-ore contract on the Dalian Commodity Exchange is 0.9% lower at CNY758.5 a ton. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

0207 GMT - Geo Energy Resources' share price may extend gains should it sustain the recent breakout on the technical chart, Phillip Securities Research's Zane Aw says in a research report. The share price broke out of an ascending triangle pattern last week accompanied by high volume, the analyst notes. Also, the share price is holding above the 5- and 10-day simple moving averages, with the two SMAs above the 20-day SMA, the analyst says. These offer potential bullish reversal signals, suggesting the share price could head higher. Resistance levels are pegged at S$0.370 and S$0.395, the analyst adds. Shares are unchanged at S$0.315. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

1021 GMT - Palm oil prices rose in the Asian trading session. The supply of crude palm oil in Malaysia is expected to remain seasonally tight, with March output likely to rise by 4% to 5%, analysts at AmInvestment Bank write in a note. Prices have been hovering between MYR4,350-MYR4,600 in the past two weeks, with trade likely to remain range-bound and cheaper prices in rival soybean oil, they add. The Bursa Malaysia Derivatives contract for June delivery rose 22 ringgit to 4,388 ringgit/ton. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

data.title: Auto & Transport Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering the Auto and Transport sector. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

0803 GMT - XPeng will likely see further growth in shipments, market-share gains and stronger margins in 2025, Nomura analysts write in a note. Demand for its cars should rise further, driven by upcoming face-lifted and new models, they add. XPeng's vehicle margin reached double digits for the first time in 4Q, thanks to accelerated shipments and cost-reduction efforts, they say. Meanwhile, the carmaker's AI push, including advanced driver-assistance systems and robotics, should further support long-term growth, they add. A potential Indonesia factory may also pave the way for long-term overseas opportunities, Nomura says. The investment bank initiates coverage of the stock with a buy rating and a target price of HK$113.00. Shares are last at HK$89.05. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

1749 GMT - The surge of imports into the nation's busiest container-port complex is slowing. The ports of Los Angeles and Long Beach in February handled the equivalent of 781,995 import containers, an 18% drop from January's record high, but 5.9% above February last year. Some retailers and manufacturers have been rushing in goods to get ahead of new tariffs on China, which were imposed in February and raised in March. Port of Los Angeles Executive Director Gene Seroka forecasts a potential 10% drop in trade activity at the port in the second half of the year because of the tariffs. (paul.berger@wsj.com)

1627 GMT - U.S. agriculture is reliant on selling exports overseas, with $105 billion of agricultural exports being shipped through ocean ports in 2024, according to the American Farm Bureau Federation — constituting 20% of total agricultural production. The proposal by President Trump to institute steep fees on Chinese-built shipping vessels may 'disproportionally' punish U.S. farmers, says the AFBF in a research note. "Farmers and ranchers, already operating on tight margins, would face higher freight rates, reduced competitiveness and potential loss of market share - all while U.S. shipbuilding capacity remains years away from scaling up," says economist Daniel Munch of the AFBF. The proposal comes amid the ongoing uncertainty around tariffs, with recommendations on reciprocal tariffs expected from the Trump Administration on April 2. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1241 GMT - Volkswagen fundamentals could become very attractive by 2026-27 should it manage to reach its earnings targets, Barclays analysts write. For 2025, guidance for up to 5% revenue growth translates to a figure of 325 billion to 341 billion euros, versus consensus at 324 billion euros, they say. The 5.5%-6.5% EBIT margin guidance implies EBIT of 20 billion euros at the mid-point versus consensus at 18.9 billion euros. The company expects the EBIT margin and free cash flow to be weak in the first quarter. The bank lifts its price target on the stock to 125 euros from 105 euros and keeps its overweight rating. Shares fall 0.7% to 110.30 euros. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0937 GMT - Rheinmetall's shares price could double to 3,000 euros by 2030 if European defense spending reaches 3% of gross domestic product, Morgan Stanley analysts Marie-Ange Riggio and Ross Law say. The German arms maker could potentially receive cumulative orders of between 325 billion euros and 450 billion euros by the end of this decade. Achieving this would force the group to double its manufacturing capacity starting in 2027, but that would be feasible, they add. The analysts estimate that Rheinmetall had a 12% share of the European equipment market in 2024, and an upward trend in market share is likely to continue as land platforms remain in focus in the near future. This could be confirmed by the European Commission's defense white paper, set to be published Wednesday. Shares are down 0.7% at 1,435 euros. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

data.title: Parke Bancorp Maintains a Quarterly Dividend of $0.18 per Share, Payable April 18 to Stockholders of Record on April 4
Moldova Industrial Output Falls the Least in 6 Months
data.description:
Industrial production in Moldova fell by 2.7% year-on-year in January 2025, easing from a 3.4% drop from the previous month.

This marked the sixth consecutive month of decline in industrial activity but the softest in the sequence, as output climbed to an almost six-year high for mining and quarrying (153% vs 13.4% in December).

At the same time, production rebounded sharply in the manufacturing sector (1.1% vs -14.2%).

On the other hand, activity decreased for electricity, gas, steam, and air conditioning supply (-9.1% vs 27.2%).

On a monthly basis, industrial output slipped by 5% in January, the first drop in five months, following a 5.2% increase in the preceding month.

data.title: Raffles Medical Group Buys Back More Shares
data.description:
Raffles Medical Group repurchased 200,000 shares in the open market on Wednesday for SG$199,260 or SG$0.995 apiece, according to a same-day filing on the Singapore Stock Exchange.

The healthcare services provider has now bought back 5.1 million shares under its current mandate.

data.title: Scholastic Keeps Quarterly Dividend of $0.20 per Share; Payable June 16 to Shareholders as of April 30
China Resources Power Sees 2025 Cash Capital Expenditure HK$56,800 Million
data.description:
China Resources Power Holdings Co Ltd :

  • CASH CAPITAL EXPENDITURE IN 2025 IS EXPECTED TO BE HK$56,800 MILLION

  • IN 2025, EXPECTS TO COMMISSION TOTAL ATTRIBUTABLE INSTALLED CAPACITY OF 6,093MW OF COAL-FIRED POWER GENERATION UNITS

data.title: European Stocks Steady Before More Central Bank Decisions
data.description:
European markets were mixed on Thursday as investors awaited interest rate decisions from the Bank of England, Swiss National Bank, and Sweden’s Riksbank.

The Stoxx 50 hovered around the flatline, while the Stoxx 600 saw slight gains after four days of increases.

Meanwhile, the U.S. Federal Reserve held rates but signaled two cuts later this year, despite uncertainty from Trump’s trade policies.

The Bank of England is expected to keep rates unchanged, while Switzerland may cut rates after low inflation.

Sweden’s Riksbank is likely to hold steady.

In corporate news, German shipping giant Hapag-Lloyd reported a 19% drop in annual profit to $2.59 billion but saw revenue growth.

The company warned of uncertainty due to volatile freight rates and geopolitical risks.

data.title: PRESSR: SalamAir renews partnership with Sabre to drive operational excellence through Radixx Solutions
data.description:
MUSCAT, Sultanate of Oman and SOUTHLAKE, Texas  – Sabre Corporation , a leading global travel technology company, today announced that SalamAir, Oman’s fastest-growing low-cost carrier, has extended its multi-year partnership with Sabre’s Radixx solutions to support its continued growth and operational efficiency. This renewal will see SalamAir continue using Radixx Res for its reservation system and Radixx Insight for reporting, further reflecting the airline’s confidence in Sabre’s Radixx solutions.

This long-term agreement provides stability and positions SalamAir for continued growth and success in an increasingly competitive airline industry.

“This renewal is an important step in our partnership with SalamAir,” said Darren Rickey, Senior Vice President of Airline Sales at Sabre. “As SalamAir continues to grow in the region, we look forward to supporting their journey with the tailored solutions Radixx offers. This extended collaboration reflects SalamAir’s trust in our ability to provide reliable, scalable technology that aligns with their operational needs and strategic goals, including the transition to Offers and Orders. We are proud to be part of their growth and success.”

SalamAir, known for offering affordable fares without compromising on service quality, has quickly become an important player in the aviation industry. With both domestic and international flights, the airline makes air travel more accessible to a wide range of customers. SalamAir’s focus on efficiency, customer satisfaction, and network expansion has fueled its rapid growth and positioned it as a preferred choice for budget-conscious travelers across Oman and beyond.

“We are pleased to continue our successful partnership with Sabre and their Radixx solutions,” said Steven Allen, Chief Commercial Officer of SalamAir. “The Radixx reservation system (Radixx Res) and data and insights platform (Radixx Insight) have been instrumental in supporting our growth, helping us innovate and deliver a seamless travel experience to our customers. This renewal reaffirms our confidence in Sabre’s solutions and their ability to adapt to our evolving needs as we expand our fleet, network, and market presence.”

Through this renewed partnership, Sabre reinforces its commitment to providing cutting-edge solutions designed to help airlines enhance their customer offering, improve operational efficiency, and support sustainable growth.

About Sabre Corporation   

Sabre Corporation is a leading technology company that takes on the biggest opportunities and solves the most complex challenges in travel. Sabre harnesses speed, scale and insights to build tomorrow’s technology today – empowering airlines, hoteliers, agencies and other partners to retail, distribute and fulfill travel worldwide. Headquartered in Southlake, Texas, USA, with employees across the world, Sabre serves customers in more than 160 countries globally. For more information visit www.sabre.com.

About SalamAir

SalamAir is Oman’s low-cost airline; SalamAir commenced operations in 2017 and focused on developing affordable air travel to Oman. Operating a fleet of 13 Airbus A320 family aircraft, SalamAir serves 6 Domestic destinations in Oman and 37 cities in 18 countries. SalamAir was voted as Oman’s Most Trusted Brand in 2022, 2023 and 2024.

SABR-F

Fatin Said

sabrenews@sabre.com

Investors

Brian Evans

data.title: Ocean Wilsons to Return Share of Wilson Son Sale Proceeds to Shareholders
data.description:
By Anthony O. Goriainoff

Ocean Wisons plans to return a portion of the $768 million funds raised from the sale of Wilson Sons to shareholders via a tender offer.

The investment company said Thursday that it would buy up to 7.1 million ordinary shares from existing shareholders via a tender offer.

The company said it plans to launch the offer shortly after the sale completes whereupon it will also disclose pricing.

Shares are up 55 pence, or 4.1%, at 1,400 pence.

The board said it will consider options on the remaining proceeds. Ocean Wilsons said it expects the deal to close by the third quarter of this year, subject to the usual conditions.

In October the company said it had agreed the sale of its 56.47% interest in Wilson Sons to SAS Shipping Agencies Services.

Write to Anthony O. Goriainoff at anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com

data.title: UK should keep price competition to drive fibre roll-out, regulator says
data.description:
Britain should keep competition in fast broadband to drive full-fibre coverage to 96% of premises by 2027, while capping the price BT's Openreach can charge for slower speeds up to 80Mbit/s, up from 40Mbits/s today, Ofcom said on Thursday.

Four years ago, the regulator encouraged companies to build gigabit-capable networks by promoting competition and by making it easier for new entrants to use ducts and telegraph poles owned by Openreach, the national network provider.

The measures helped drive the roll-out of full fibre networks from less than a quarter of premises to nearly seven in 10, Ofcom said.

It proposed keeping the price of high speed products free from regulation for five years from 2026, while protecting consumers using copper-fibre connections of up to 80Mbit/s by capping the price Openreach can charge to retail providers such as Sky and TalkTalk.

In the most remote parts of the country where new commercial networks were less viable, it said it would promote investment in full-fibre build by Openreach.

Shares in BT opened up 0.5% on Thursday.

Ofcom will consult on its proposals until June 12, and intends to publish its decisions in March 2026.

data.title: Market Chatter: Hon Hai Sees AI Server Revenue Surpassing iPhone Sales
data.description:
Hon Hai Precision Industry , better known as Foxconn, expects its AI server revenue to top iPhone sales within two years, Taipei Times reported Thursday, citing chairman Young Liu's address at Nvidia Corp's annual technology conference.

The company unveiled its new AI server racks, powered by Nvidia's latest Blackwell GPUs and Grace CPUs, as part of its focus on AI infrastructure. Hon Hai's unit Ingrasys is providing liquid cooling solutions for next-gen AI servers, with production ramping up after starting small-batch output in December, the publication said.

Hon Hai also debuted its AI-powered nursing robot, Nurabot, set to be deployed at hospitals in Taiwan later in 2025, the report added.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)
